session_hint: the React dashboard unnecessary re-renders investigation
question: What approaches did we rule out when trying to fix the React dashboard's unnecessary re-renders?
turns: The dashboard page re-renders every component tree on every keystroke in a search box. Profiler shows ~200ms render time. First theory: the Redux store update is touching too many slices.
Audited Redux: the search action only updates one slice and all selectors are memoized with reselect. Component subscriptions are minimal. Redux is not causing the cascade, ruling it out.
Tried wrapping the expensive child components in React.memo. Made no difference — they're still re-rendering. Checked props: they look stable but something is referentially different on every render.

Found the root cause: the ThemeContext.Provider is at the top of the tree and its value prop is an inline object literal — { theme, toggleTheme } — created fresh on every render of the root component. Every consumer re-renders because the reference always changes. Moved the value to a useMemo and re-renders dropped to zero.
